instruction: Configure terminal size to be persistent with 140 columns and 46 rows. Also create a backup of ~/.bashrc as ~/.bashrc.osworld.bak before applying changes. The setting must take effect in a newly opened terminal automatically.
source: 外部参考来源：Canonical tutorial - Command line for beginners (https://ubuntu.com/tutorials/command-line-for-beginners)

parameters.command: cp ~/.bashrc ~/.bashrc.osworld.seed.bak >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; sed -i '/stty rows 46 cols 140/d' ~/.bashrc 2>/dev/null || true; rm -f ~/.bashrc.osworld.bak

command: import pyautogui, time; time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl','alt','t'); time.sleep(0.6); pyautogui.write('stty size'); time.sleep(0.3); pyautogui.press('enter')

result.command: backup_ok='false'; [ -f ~/.bashrc.osworld.bak ] && backup_ok='true'; count=$(grep -c 'stty rows 46 cols 140' ~/.bashrc 2>/dev/null || echo 0); bashrc_ok='false'; [ "$count" -eq 1 ] && bashrc_ok='true'; size=$(stty size 2>/dev/null || echo '0 0'); size_ok='false'; echo "$size" | grep -q '46 140' && size_ok='true'; echo "backup_ok=$backup_ok"; echo "bashrc_ok=$bashrc_ok"; echo "size_ok=$size_ok"
